approach.reasoning: The agent correctly framed the problem and followed the suggested approach: they queried the ERCOT database, inspected available zone names, constructed a month-aggregated query that filters for day-ahead prices and 2023 timestamps, and used GROUP BY date_trunc('month') to compute monthly averages. Tool usage is appropriate (listing tables, checking zone names, explaining the query plan, then executing the aggregation). The timestamp bounds and is_day_ahead filter are appropriate, and rounding the result is a reasonable presentation step. No substantive flaws or omissions were identified.

accuracy.reasoning: All 12 monthly values supplied by the agent match the ground truth within the allowed tolerance (<= 2 USD absolute error). Per-month absolute differences: Jan 0.27, Feb 0.59, Mar 0.15, Apr 0.19, May 0.25, Jun 0.22, Jul 1.92, Aug 1.17, Sep 0.83, Oct 0.46, Nov 0.11, Dec 0.08. Units (USD/MWh), time basis (monthly averages for 2023), market (ERCOT Houston hub) and product (day-ahead prices) are correctly stated. The agent's answer is complete and factually aligned with the provided ground truth.

sources.reasoning: The agent provided a full set of monthly averages but did not cite any data source or method of computation. For energy market price claims, authoritative citations are required: acceptable sources include ERCOT Market Information (MIS) LMP/daily/DA data, an internal ERCOT/GridStatus API database (stated as equivalent to external market portals), or a known market data vendor (e.g., S&P Global, ICAP, ABB/MarketMap) that publishes ERCOT DA hub prices. The presented numbers are plausible in pattern (notably very high August DA avg) but cannot be validated without a referenced data feed or a description of the aggregation method (time zone, settlement intervals used, treatment of missing values). Because the answer omitted explicit sources and methodology, it is insufficient for production use. Recommendation: cite ERCOT MIS (or the specific internal GridStatus/market database endpoint), include the exact data series name (Houston Hub DA prices), date/time resolution used (e.g., hourly DA LMPs), and the aggregation method (monthly mean of hourly values), and provide the raw data or script/CSV so the averages can be independently verified.
